Notice Of And Motion To Bifurcate
Off Calendar Sep-21-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Robert Platt, Esq. 310-312-4221 and Sharon Bauman, Esq. 310-312-4327 of Manatt for Plaintiffs.
Michael Von Loewenfeldt, Esq. and Frank Busch, Esq. 415-357-8500 of Kerr & Wagstaff for Defendant Dean Luca Krause.

RULING - A R G U E D. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

PLAINTIFF POLYMATHIC PROPERTIES, INC., MEYER CORPORATION, U.S.'s Motion To Bifurcate.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling. On September 12, 2018, the parties stipulated to allow plaintiffs to file a third amended complaint and a second amended answer to the cross-complaint in this action. The motions to permit those amended pleadings are GRANTED.

From review of the new pleadings, I note that they appear to be a wholesale rewrite of the prior pleadings. I also note defendant's statement that, while stipulating to filing, he does "not agree that any of the claims asserted in these documents are properly pleaded" or "timely." This strongly suggests that motions on the pleadings are in the offing. I therefore STAY all other pending motions - including all discovery motions and the motion to bifurcate - until the pleadings are settled and the case's scope can be ascertained. Any party may then move to lift the stay.

Counsel for plaintiff to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr., Clerk: M.Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, torreanom@aol.com, Reported. =(302/RBU)

Plaintiffs' Motion For Protective Order
Off Calendar Aug-31-2017 Denied
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Plaintiffs' Polymathic Properties, Inc., and Meyer Corporation's Motion For Protective Order

Plaintiffs Polymathic Properties, Inc. and Meyer Corporation U.S.'s motion for protective order is denied. The April 26, 2017 preliminary injunction order was only meant to restrict defendants Dean Krause and Cheuk Chiu's dissemination and use of documents to non-parties and for use other than in litigation between and among the parties and public dissemination of assertedly confidential material in litigation between and among the parties. That order was not intended to, and does not, preclude or limit discovery propounded by the parties on each other that was otherwise permissible by the discovery statutes. Confidentiality of discovery and responses thereto propounded by the parties on each other can and should be handled by a confidentiality protective order and, if necessary, motions to seal.

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Gordon Aiavao, CSR#11216, gaiavao@sftc.org, Reported. =(302/HK)
